characters.name: Ally McBeal
characters.actor_name: Calista FlockhartAmy Castle(Young Ally)Dakota Fanning(5 Year Old Ally)Mika Boorem(7 Year Old Ally)Ashley Marie(12-14 Year Old Ally)
characters.description: Ally McBealBornAllison Marie McBealBirthdayApril 1970Her birthday is the same week asJohn Cage[1]Nickname(s)AllyThemeTell Him,Oooh ChildOccupationSenior Administrative Partner atCage, Fish & McBeal(Formerly)Associate atCage & Fish(Formerly)RelationshipsFamilyJeannie McBeal(mother)George McBeal(father)Unnamed Woman †(sister)Unnamed Man(brother)Maddie Harrington(biological daughter)RomancesBilly Thomas(high school sweetheart/ex-boyfriend)Professor James Dawson(affair)Glenn(fling)Dr. Greg Butters(ex-boyfriend)Brian Selig(ex-boyfriend)Larry Paul(ex-boyfriend)Victor Morrison(ex-boyfriend)Joel(fling)FriendsRenée Raddick•John Cage•Elaine VassalGeorgia Thomas•Richard FishBackstagePortrayed byCalista FlockhartAmy Castle(Young Ally)Dakota Fanning(5 Year Old Ally)Mika Boorem(7 Year Old Ally)Ashley Marie(12-14 Year Old Ally)First seenLast seenPilotBygonesEpisodes112Ally McBeal, full name Allison Marie McBeal, is the protagonist ofAlly McBeal, portrayed byCalista Flockhart. Ally is a major romantic who refuses to believe that love can't triumph all. She often finds herself working on cases where love, men vs women, and social justice are key issues. She is easily distracted by cases where there are romantic notions and can find a way to relate them back to her personal life. She is quirky, awkward, and in a constant swirl of laughable drama all while searching for love and personal happiness. She is quirky, awkward, and in a constant swirl of laughable drama all while searching for love and personal happiness.

characters.name: Billy Thomas
characters.description: Billy ThomasBornWilliam Allen ThomasOccupationLawyer atCage & FishRelationshipsRomancesSandy Hingle(ex-girlfriend)Georgia Thomas(ex-wife)Ally McBeal(affair/ex-girlfriend/high school sweetheart)FriendsAlly McBeal•Richard Fish•John CageBackstagePortrayed byGil BellowsNicholas Pappone(Age 7)Michael Galeota(Billy age 12-14)First seenLast seenPilotBygonesEpisodes66Billy Thomasis a principal character onAlly McBeal, portrayed byGil Bellows. Billy is a competent lawyer and very conservative guy. He's sexist and occasionally a good friend to some of his colleagues. Billy grew up with Ally and they first kissed as kids. They were together in high school and she followed him to Harvard Law where they stayed together for a year until Billy decided to move to Michigan after having met Georgia while visiting the school there.[1]He didn't tell Ally that, but used the importance of clerking in Michigan as a reason for the break-up. In 1996, Billy married Georgia. At his bachelor party, which was attended byJudge Dennis 'Happy' Boyle, there was a sex worker whom Billy slept with.[2] Billy is a competent lawyer and very conservative guy. He's sexist and occasionally a good friend to some of his colleagues.
characters.personality_section: Billy is a competent lawyer and very conservative guy. He's sexist and occasionally a good friend to some of his colleagues.

characters.description: Caroline PoopOccupationAttorneyBackstagePortrayed bySandra BernhardFirst seenLast seenDrawing the LinesThe Dirty JokeCaroline Poopis an attorney appearing in two episodes of the first season of Ally McBeal. Season OneShe first appeared to representElaine Vassalafter she put in a complaint of sexual harassment. She then represented the delivery girl for sexual harassment in the following episode. She describesGeorgia ThomasandAlly McBealas Skipper and Barbie dolls.

characters.name: Dr. Tracey Clark
characters.description: Tracey ClarkOccupationPsychiatristBackstagePortrayed byTracey UllmanFirst seenLast seenTheme of LifeTroubled WaterEpisodes5Dr. Tracey Clarkis a recurring character onAlly McBeal, portrayed byTracey Ullman. Season OneTracey was first introduced in the episodeTheme of LifewhereAllyfinally acceptedJohn Cage's advice to see his therapist. Ally described to the therapist Dr. Tracey Clark how she started seeing theDancing Babyand how he acts. Tracey used unconventional methods making Ally think she wasn't a trained psychiatrist. Tracey asked aboutBillyand told Ally she did wishGeorgiasome harm and advice her to strike Georgia during their upcoming kickboxing match. Ally did so, resulting both she and Georgia falling flat down and Billy rushing into the ring, unsure who to aid. Tracey next suggested that Ally needed a theme song to make herself better. She introduces Ally to her theme song, 'Tracy' by The Cufflinks dances and sings to it, with Ally further distrusting Tracey. Upon returning, Ally initial sings what she claims is a theme song but actually is the series' titles theme. Tracey immediately remarked it sucks and ask for another and Ally choses 'Tell Him' written by Bert Berns and pouplarised by The Exciters. Tracey accepted that song and told Ally she would feel better with in in her head. Season Two
